strengths: Roughly 19 years of hands-on software engineering across web, mobile, embedded, and high-throughput backend systems, exceeding the 15-year minimum
Founder experience (GISCollective, 2016–present) and recent work on AI-agent infrastructure, MCP code generators, and agentic tooling at re:cinq
Track record of knowledge sharing through conference talks, multi-day courses, and sustained open-source library maintenance
concerns: No evidence of director-level or principal-level scope: career titles top out at Senior/Staff developer with individual-contributor delivery work, not organization-wide technical strategy

AI exposure is shallow and recent (~1 year of agent tooling since April 2025); no ML/AI research background, no advanced degree, and no recognized AI expertise or shipped AI products at scale as the JD prefers
No demonstrated experience advising strategic customers or executives, setting cloud product direction, or influencing engineering strategy beyond team-level contributions

key_factors.factor: Thin, recent AI expertise vs. recognized-expert requirement

response.justification: The candidate is a versatile, seasoned hands-on engineer, but the role demands a director-level individual contributor who shapes AI and cloud strategy, advises strategic customers, and carries recognized AI credibility. His resume shows team-level delivery roles, a small self-run open-source platform rather than entrepreneurship inside a large organization, and AI involvement limited to about a year of agent tooling with no research pedigree, published AI products, or platform-strategy experience. The gap between senior implementer and principal-level AI strategist is substantial, making him a weak fit for this position.
